rationale: Approved v1 product design and breakdown are migrated unchanged.
Backend-only bounded implementation on existing files.

resolutions.decision: Use 1800 seconds and require the effective scheduled slow-private transport TTL to remain <=1800.
Separate scheduled Group A/B/C source updates from cache-only assembly and keep interestRateHistory selected-asset fallback-only.
Use the strict homepage borrow predicate, remove the global top-50 cap, and exclude low-rate rows from scheduled borrow work and coverage.

design_conflict_override.reason: The registered Codex and Claude decision providers both have prior design-stage involvement and no unrelated registered review-2 decision provider is enabled. The human selected Opus4.8; Anthropic is provider-isolated from all delivery-code authors and uses the breakdown-involvement disclosure override.

residual_risks: P3: Group A panel business due uses cache_ttl_seconds while private transport uses private_channel_fast_ttl_seconds; defaults are 60/60 and this stage does not change the configurable divergence contract.

P3: inherited _fetch_history_for writes funding_history_cache with the function-entry monotonic time rather than fetch completion time; review-1 found no separate transport-cache masquerade risk and did not require a fix.

user_acceptance.source: User explicitly requested the reviewed scheduler code be merged and pushed, then narrowed the immediate corrective scope to history refresh-due at 1500 seconds only. The v2 merge is used as the reviewed local main baseline; remote push waits for the corrective stage.
